questions.question: What is an election?
questions.answer: An election is a process through which people choose their representatives in a democracy.

questions.question: Why are elections necessary in a democracy?
questions.answer: Elections are necessary because they allow people to choose their leaders and change them if they do not perform well.

questions.question: What is political competition?
questions.answer: Political competition is the contest among different political parties and candidates to win elections.

questions.question: Mention one advantage of political competition.
questions.answer: Political competition encourages leaders to work for the welfare of the people.

questions.question: What is a constituency?
questions.answer: A constituency is a geographical area that elects one representative to a legislative body.

questions.question: What is the voters list?
questions.answer: The voters list is the official list of all eligible voters in a constituency.

questions.question: What is universal adult franchise?
questions.answer: Universal adult franchise means every citizen aged 18 years or above has the right to vote.

questions.question: What are reserved constituencies?
questions.answer: Reserved constituencies are seats reserved for candidates belonging to Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes.

questions.question: What is an election campaign?
questions.answer: An election campaign is when political parties and candidates promote their ideas and policies to attract voters.

questions.question: What is polling?
questions.answer: Polling is the process of casting votes in an election.

questions.question: What are Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs)?
questions.answer: Electronic Voting Machines are devices used to record votes during elections.

questions.question: Who conducts elections in India?
questions.answer: The Election Commission of India conducts elections in the country.

questions.question: Write one function of the Election Commission.
questions.answer: The Election Commission ensures free and fair elections.

questions.question: What is a by-election?
questions.answer: A by-election is an election held to fill a vacant seat in a constituency.

questions.question: What is a general election?
questions.answer: A general election is held to elect representatives for all constituencies.

questions.question: What does MP stand for?
questions.answer: MP stands for Member of Parliament.

questions.question: What does MLA stand for?
questions.answer: MLA stands for Member of Legislative Assembly.

questions.question: What is the minimum voting age in India?
questions.answer: The minimum voting age in India is 18 years.

questions.question: What is the Model Code of Conduct?
questions.answer: It is a set of rules that political parties and candidates must follow during elections.

questions.question: What happens after votes are counted?
questions.answer: The candidate who gets the highest number of votes is declared the winner.

questions.question: What is voter turnout?
questions.answer: Voter turnout is the percentage of eligible voters who actually cast their vote.

questions.question: What is NOTA?
questions.answer: NOTA stands for None of the Above.

questions.question: Give one example of an election slogan used in India.
questions.answer: One example is 'Garibi Hatao' used by the Congress Party in the 1971 election.

questions.question: Why are elections held regularly?
questions.answer: Elections are held regularly so that people can evaluate and replace their leaders.

questions.question: What ensures free and fair elections in India?
questions.answer: An independent Election Commission ensures free and fair elections.
